spenser_killer_insight: the node group is exposed here like a capsule pretty much — you can drag custom geo into the AM and control all of the params without going into the node graph
purpose: Flagship artist-grade procedural mycelium growth tool, structured AS A CAPSULE. Drag any custom geometry into AM Source Object slot, tune 21 named AM parameters, never opens the graph. Outputs voxelized mycelium-on-surface via Volume Builder + Volume Mesher + Smoothing.
visual_result: Multi-color age-encoded mycelium mat (green to yellow to orange to blue/purple gradient) covers Source Object surface. Each tip is a 3D bumpy cluster; surface gradually filled by f30-40.

test_objects_demonstrated.name: Test_Sphere
test_objects_demonstrated.outcome: DEFAULT — full multi-color mat covering sphere surface

test_objects_demonstrated.name: Test Disc
test_objects_demonstrated.outcome: WORKS via UI Play — flat disc with 3D mycelium bumps growing across; Python rewind+step did NOT reproduce reliably (gotcha noted)

test_objects_demonstrated.name: Test Multi Disc
test_objects_demonstrated.outcome: Spenser confirmed: works fine via UI Play. Python attempt produced 0 mesher points — Python SetTime+ExecutePasses does not equivalently trigger memory@ reset.

memory@.role: per-frame growth state — positions, ages, alive flags

surfacebluenoise@.role: initial spore distribution on Source Object

active@ + invertselection@ + delete@.role: LIFECYCLE PRUNING — selects alive tips, inverts to dead, deletes dead. Bounds memory growth.

if@ × 3.role: DEFENSIVE GATES for unbound/wrong-type Source Object

group@ Fast Spline Sweep.role: INTERNAL SPLINE SWEEP — graph-level spline-to-mesh, bypasses classic OM Sweep

buildfromsinglevalue@ Age + maprange@ Ra.role: age normalization to [0,1] for vertex map

legacyobjectaccess@ Connect.role: OM bridge for Source Object binding

disc@ Disc.role: internal fallback surface if Source Object unbound

om_classic_generators.name: Test_Sphere / Test Disc / Test Multi Disc
om_classic_generators.role: pre-baked test surfaces for Source Object binding

what_is_clever: CAPSULE FORM — 21 AM params let artists drag custom geo and tune everything via AM. The graph is hidden. This is the gold standard for shippable Scene Nodes.
Three Plain falloff fields as host children for age-driven parameter modulation (Lift / Scale / Displace by Age).
Lifecycle pruning chain (active + invertselection + delete) bounds memory growth as old tips die.
Defensive `if@ If GeometryObject` gates handle unbound/wrong-type Source Object gracefully.
Internal Fast Spline Sweep group bypasses classic OM Sweep — graph emits mesh directly.
Age vertex map painted via set_property@ Set Weights — encodes age as vertex weight for shader-driven color/opacity.
Age normalization via buildfromsinglevalue + maprange remaps 0..MaxAge to 0..1 for material consumption.
Surface Blue-Noise sampling for non-aliased spore distribution — better than uniform random for natural-looking distribution.

gotchas_documented.issue: Python SetTime+ExecutePasses does NOT reliably trigger memory@ reset on parameter swap. UI Play works; Python-only attempt produced 0 output for Test Multi Disc.
gotchas_documented.evidence: Swapped Source Object to Test Multi Disc, rewound to f0 in Python, stepped 0..30 — mesher cache = 0 points. Spenser confirmed UI Play works: 'the multi disc and sphere work fine if you do that'.
gotchas_documented.workaround: When studying memory@ scenes, ask user to press UI Play. Don't conclude 'broken' from Python eval alone. cinema4d-mcp needs a true UI-Play-equivalent helper for memory@ scenes.

gotchas_documented.issue: Memory needs time to build up. Quote from Spenser: 'anything with MEMORY probably needs the memory to build up' — you can't jump to f40 and expect built-up state.
gotchas_documented.workaround: Sequential warmup from f0 is mandatory. UI Play is the most reliable trigger.

rebuild_recipe: See study.md for full recipe (48-node graph + 21 AM params + 3 fields + Volume pipeline). This is a flagship capsule recipe — implementation is heavy but every primitive is documented in the recipe library after this study.

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: artist_grade_growth_tool (capsule)
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Production-grade procedural growth tool. Drag custom geo into Source Object slot, tune 21 params via AM, no graph editing required. Same skeleton serves vines, ivy, root systems, hair, lightning, branching coral, neural networks, tree growth.
secondary_recipes: R42_time_driven_procedural_growth — root.time as input, no memory@ needed (scene 13)
blockers_to_recipe_implementation: Python-side memory@ reset on param swap is unreliable (UI Play needed) — cinema4d-mcp helper required
Internal Fast Spline Sweep group implementation is non-trivial
21 typed floatingio synthesis with semantic effectivename labels
Plain falloff field children of host needs API surface

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: CAPSULE form = artist-shipping endpoint. Recipe library MUST distinguish bare-graph vs capsule recipes. Tag every recipe with recipe_style.
Python SetTime+ExecutePasses is NOT equivalent to UI Play for memory@ scenes. Need a true UI-Play helper.
48-node graph is the shippable-tool sweet spot — not too sparse (research-only), not too bloated.
21 AM params is the artist-fluency bar for production-ready capsules.
Defensive if@ gates + lifecycle pruning + internal spline sweep are universal capsule-quality features.
